channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Olivia Hussey, star of 1968 film 'Romeo and Juliet,' dies at 73
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Olivia Hussey, the actor who starred as a teenage Juliet in the 1968 film “Romeo and Juliet,” has died, her family said on social media Saturday. She was 73. Hussey died on Friday, “peacefully at home surrounded by her loved ones,” a statement posted to her Instagram account said.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Serial killer Richard Ramirez’s bride a ‘superfan’ who put herself ‘top of the line’ for his love: pal
item.description: Richard Ramirez, the serial killer who was sentenced to death following his heinous crimes in California, died while on death row in 2013. He was 53.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A Mega Millions player in California is $1.22B richer
item.description: After no players won on Christmas Eve, the Mega Millions lottery jackpot ballooned to $1.22 billion. The winning ticket was sold Friday night.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Soriano, NewsNation

item.title: Winning ticket for $1.22 billion lottery jackpot sold in California, Mega Millions says
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — At least one Mega Millions player has plenty of dough to ring in the New Year after drawing the winning number.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Bird flu virus in Louisiana patient likely mutated to be more transmissible, CDC says
item.description: The agency, in its latest update released Thursday, said the mutations were not found in samples taken from wild birds, suggesting the virus changed over the course of the patient’s infection.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, KTLA 5

item.title: Missing dog returned to family home and rang the doorbell
item.description: Athena, a 4-year-old Husky and German Shepherd mix, found her way back to her Florida family home and even rang the doorbell after a nearly weeklong search.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Azerbaijani and U.S. officials suggest plane that crashed may have been hit by weapons fire
item.description: Russian air defense systems may have brought down an Azerbaijani airliner this week, a U.S. official said Friday after an Azerbaijani minister also suggested the plane was hit by a weapon, citing expert analysis and survivor accounts.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Woman opens emergency exit, climbs onto wing of Alaska Airlines plane in Seattle
item.description: A woman was taken to a hospital for evaluation after she climbed onto the wing of an Alaska Airlines plane on Sunday while it deplaning at a gate, according to officials.
item.dc:creator: Kevin Accettulla, WFLA, NBC

item.title: An online debate over foreign workers in tech shows tensions in Trump's political coalition
item.description: WEST PALM BEACH, Fla. (AP) — An online spat between factions of Donald Trump's supporters over immigration and the tech industry has thrown internal divisions in his political movement into public display, previewing the fissures and contradictory views his coalition could bring to the White House.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE L. PRICE, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Final crystal triangles installed on Times Square ball ahead of New Year's Eve
item.description: The last of the crystal triangles that make up this year’s Times Square New Year’s Eve ball have been installed, marking the first time in 10 years that all 2,688 were replaced at once.
item.dc:creator: By Melissa Goldin, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Another unticketed passenger attempts to stow away on a Delta flight
item.description: In the thick of holiday travel, another unticketed passenger attempted to stow away on a Delta Air Lines flight.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pennsylvania teacher jailed in Russia formally designated as wrongfully detained
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — An American schoolteacher arrested in Russia on drug charges more than four years ago has been designated by the U.S. government as wrongfully detained, the State Department said Friday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Onion-wielding man disrupts Christmas church services, pours whiskey into holy water: deputies
item.description: A Maryland man was arrested and charged with several offences stemming from disruptions at two Catholic churches on Christmas Eve, including one disruption which he is accused of pouring liquor into a container of holy water.
item.dc:creator: Alaina Papazian, ABC 4

item.title: SEE IT: ‘Peanut-shaped’ roundabout ready to open in Ohio
item.description: In a post to the city's Facebook page, officials offered "a few pointers to help drivers navigate safely.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Langenfeld, ABC 4

item.title: China sanctions 7 companies over US military assistance to Taiwan
item.description: The sanctions also come in response to the recent approval of the U.S. government's annual defense spending bill, which a Chinese Foreign Ministry statement said “includes multiple negative sections on China.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: US to send $1.25 billion in weapons to Ukraine, pushing to get aid out before Biden leaves office
item.description: U.S. officials say the United States is expected to announce it will send another $1.25 billion in military assistance to Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Lolita C. Baldor And Matthew Lee, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Judge scolds Jay-Z's lawyer over 'attacks' on rape accuser's attorney
item.description: Judge Analisa Torres chastised the lawyer representing Jay-Z for what she described as his combative motions and “inflammatory language” against the plaintiff's lawyer,
item.dc:creator: Larry Neumeister, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: These movies are some of 2025's most anticipated
item.description: With the vast majority of 2024 in the rearview mirror, it's time to start looking at the most anticipated films primed to take the silver screen in 2025. Full of adaptations, sequels, and reboots of beloved properties, viewers will not have difficulty finding something familiar to enjoy.
item.dc:creator: Justin Walker, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: VIDEO: Dog, man stranded in rough waters get rescued on Christmas
item.description: A Christmas morning boat trip for a man and his dog turned into a rescue mission after they got stranded in the Pacific Ocean's rough seas.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, WFLA, NBC

item.title: LA building from famous rock album cover goes up in flames
item.description: Once featured on the cover of a Doors album, the former site of the Morrison Hotel was destroyed by a fire on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Cameron Kiszla, NewsNation

item.title: How Trump changed America’s legal landscape
item.description: President-elect Trump's legal issues dominated his 2024 presidential bid, where courtroom appearances were just as common as rallies and campaign stops.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Musk stirs pot on X over 'shortage' of talented American engineers
item.description: Musk, an immigrant from South Africa, posted to X that there is a shortage of "talented" engineers in the U.S. and companies should start looking to other countries.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Soriano, NewsNation

item.title: DOGE committee will look for ways to slash trillions in spending
item.description: The year may be winding down but for Team Trump, the transition to the White House is in high gear, that includes tech billionaire Elon Musk's DOGE initiative to dismantle parts of the federal government.
item.dc:creator: Raquel Martin, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Most blame insurance profits and denials alongside the killer in UHC CEO death: poll
item.description: Most Americans believe health insurance profits and coverage denials share responsibility for the killing of UnitedHealthcare's CEO — although not as much as the person who pulled the trigger, according to a new poll.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Get off my lawn! 5 times squatters took advantage of unwitting homeowners in 2024
item.description: A string of high-profile squatter cases have pushed multiple states to pass legislation to protect homeowners this year.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Man arrested for disrupting Christmas church services, pouring whiskey into holy water: deputies
item.description: Authorities in Maryland said a man has been arrested after he disrupted services at two local churches on Christmas Eve and endangered citizens.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Getting too close to first responders will soon be a crime in Florida
item.description: Florida will soon have a so-called “Halo Law” meant to protect first responders. The new law, starting in January, creates a 25 feet buffer zone around law enforcement officers, firefighters, and emergency medical crews.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, NewsNation

item.title: Los Angeles-area authorities investigating human legs found on shoreline
item.description: Authorities in Southern California found human remains along a rocky shoreline but have not determined the identity of the person, according to reports.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Retailers, chains that announced closures in 2024
item.description: It was another tough year for restaurants and traditional brick-and-mortar retailers. Several national chains announced closures and bankruptcies in 2024 – not to mention the local small businesses that quietly shuttered around the country.
item.dc:creator: Celeste Houmard, KTLA 5

item.title: Man arraigned on murder charges in NYC subway death fanned flames with a shirt, prosecutors say
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The man accused of burning a woman to death inside a New York City subway train used a shirt to fan the flames, a prosecutor said Tuesday at his arraignment on murder charges. Sebastian Zapeta, 33, who federal immigration officials said is a Guatemalan citizen who entered the U.S.
item.dc:creator: CEDAR ATTANASIO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Biden signs a bill officially making the bald eagle the national bird of the US
item.description: The bald eagle, a symbol of the power and strength of the United States for more than 240 years, earned an overdue honor on Tuesday: It officially became the country's national bird.
item.dc:creator: DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Mega Millions: Jackpot now over $1 billion
item.description: The Mega Millions jackpot has only been won three times in 2024, the fewest wins in a single year since the game began.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Opinion: Elon Musk wants to ‘delete’ many Americans' financial lifeline
item.description: It is especially surprising that one of the first federal agencies to come under scrutiny from the incoming administration is one that has returned billions of dollars to many of the same consumers who were counting on leaders in Washington to look out for their wallets.
item.dc:creator: Sharon McGowan, opinion contributor, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Bill Clinton discharged after hospitalization for flu
item.description: Former President Clinton was discharged Tuesday morning after being hospitalized a day earlier for flu, according to his spokesperson.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5

item.title: Major storm pounds California's central coast, blamed for man's death and partially collapsing pier
item.description: SANTA CRUZ, Calif. (AP) — A major storm pounded California’s central coast on Monday, bringing flooding and high surf that was blamed for fatally trapping a man beneath debris on a beach and later partially collapsing a pier, tossing three people into the Pacific Ocean.
item.dc:creator: MARTHA MENDOZA and STEFANIE DAZIO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Inside the state and federal charges against the man accused of killing UnitedHealthcare’s CEO
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A triptych of criminal charges paints a searing, sometimes disparate portrait of the man accused of ambushing and killing UnitedHealthcare’s CEO Brian Thompson as the executive arrived at a Manhattan hotel for his company’s annual investor conference.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Luigi Mangione's lawyer blasts New York officials for 'staged' perp walk
item.description: The attorney for Luigi Mangione, who has been accused of killing UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson, slammed New York City officials for what she referred to as a “staged” perp walk for her client last week.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, ABC 4
